alternatives.what_would_falsify_it: A normalized-action theorem requiring Hermitian adjoint-pair transport.
alternatives.what_would_select_it: An action theorem proving CKM transport is oriented one-way before Hermitian completion.
alternatives.why_it_might_apply: The CKM target is an up/down charged-current cross-sector map.
alternatives.why_it_might_not_apply: A real/Hermitian charged-current action would normally include the adjoint direction.

alternatives.what_would_falsify_it: An action theorem selecting a different CKM transport space or proving the h.c. term is not CKM-counting input.
alternatives.what_would_select_it: A normalized BHSM action derivation selecting the off-diagonal Hermitian charged-current adjoint pair.
alternatives.why_it_might_apply: The charged-current target is off-diagonal and contains an explicit Hermitian-conjugate term.
alternatives.why_it_might_not_apply: The located evidence is target-level; normalized action selection remains missing.

alternatives.what_would_falsify_it: A normalized-action theorem selecting the adjoint-pair or another non-self-response space.
alternatives.what_would_select_it: New action evidence reviving maximal self-response as the CKM transport source.
alternatives.why_it_might_apply: It has the same numerical dimension, 16.
alternatives.why_it_might_not_apply: Same numerical dimension does not prove same physical source; End(V_d) is a self-response, not the off-diagonal CKM target.

alternatives.what_would_falsify_it: A theorem restricting CKM transport to cross-sector charged-current blocks.
alternatives.what_would_select_it: An action theorem showing CKM transport averages over all sector self-responses.
alternatives.why_it_might_apply: It captures all sectorwise charged self-responses.
alternatives.why_it_might_not_apply: It includes diagonal self blocks rather than the off-diagonal CKM charged-current pair.

alternatives.what_would_falsify_it: A theorem selecting the smaller charged-current adjoint pair or one-way block.
alternatives.what_would_select_it: An action theorem proving CKM transport acts on the full charged endomorphism algebra.
alternatives.why_it_might_apply: It is the complete charged endomorphism algebra.
alternatives.why_it_might_not_apply: It includes non-CKM diagonal and cross-sector blocks and has no current action selection.

claim_boundary: The maximal self-response channel also has dimension 16, but it is retired as the primary CKM source unless action evidence revives it.
same_dimension_warning: Same numerical dimension does not prove same physical source.
